match.userMessage:
Modern social-first product photography with tactile editorial energy frames SCARLETT in light casual beach clothing suitable for walking while carrying a canvas tote bag as she bends forward at the waist with arm extended and muscles tensed mid-reach to retrieve the metallic tube from (CORAL_LIPSTICK) lying on wet sand at the Bondi shoreline, water streaming off the metal as the wave recedes leaving delicate foam trails across beige sand and shallow turquoise wash, dynamic ocean edge with gentle rolling waves and white foam in the midground, bright natural daylight creating crisp directional shadows sparkling reflections on water and glossy metal surfaces, high-key airy atmosphere with warm highlights and immediate real light, shallow depth of field keeping the action and product interaction tack sharp against softly blurred sea and horizon, eye-level handheld camera with slight downward angle for immersive natural framing, clean bright color grading with true-to-life tones and one accent color popping against neutral sand and water.

  <reference-images>
    - Image 1 [CHARACTER REF]: Scarlett - Match the real-world appearance of Sienna Blake exactly
    - Image 2 [LOCATION REF]: EXT. BONDI BEACH SHORELINE - DAY - Wet sand shoreline at Bondi Beach where waves gently wash over bare feet
    - Image 3 [ELEMENT REF]: CORAL_LIPSTICK - A slim metallic lipstick tube containing a vivid bright coral bullet
    
    IMPORTANT: Reference images CARRY the visual identity of their labeled objects. When the prompt names an UPPERCASE element token, render it faithfully from its ELEMENT REF image — do not generate a new version from the prose. When the prompt names a LOCATION REF, use that image for the environment, lighting, and architectural identity. Prose describes how things are framed, lit, and positioned in the shot — it does not redefine what the reference already shows.
  </reference-images>
